rulings.Case Title: JOHN HENNESSY VS. YELLOWPAGES.COM LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Yellowpages.Com Llc'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication (Part 2 Of 2 For Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 26, 2015, Line 3, Defendant Yellowpages.Com LLC'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication (Part 2 Of 2 For Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only). (Continued from Part 1 of 2) The court denies summary adjudication on the Labor Code sec. 970 cause of action. At paragraph 5 of his declaration, Plaintiff declares that he sought to move based on defendant's representations, not his son's lacrosse future. Plaintiff's movement to rental property prior to the termination could support the cause of action. The court denies summary adjudication as to the claim for punitive damages. Defendant's separate statement focuses on Mr. Collins and states that he was not an officer, director, or managing agent. The defendant does not sufficiently show that Ms. Gentry was not a managing agent. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SRB)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN HENNESSY VS. YELLOWPAGES.COM LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 26, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT YELLOWPAGES.COM LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication (Part 1 of 2 For Purposes of Entry of Tentative Ruling Only). The motion for summary judgment/adjudication is denied in its entirety. Defendant failed to maintain its burden of production. The court cannot rule as a matter of law that the unclean hands or after-acquired evidence doctrines completely bar plaintiff's claims. First, the defenses raise factual issues that the trier of fact should resolve. Plaintiff correctly cites Ford v. Sup. Crt. (1959) 176 Cal.App.2d 754, 759, for the proposition that a jury will resolve the unclean hands defense because plaintiff brings an action at law. Plaintiff's declaration (pars. 18-24) explains that his role in the resurrection of Pallina Partners was limited and could even benefit defendant. Second, in Camp v. Jeffer, Mangels, Butler & Marmaro (1995) 35 Cal.App.4th 620, 638, the court explained that the after-acquired evidence doctrine could bar a claim in its entirety-rather than merely limit damages-where the employee submitted a false employment application and government-imposed requirements disqualified the plaintiff from employment. This action does not involve government-imposed requirements. The court denies summary adjudication on the breach of contract and breach of implied covenant causes of action. See Sheppard v. Morgan Keegan & Co. (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 61, 67. Even if Guz v. Bechtel Inc. (2004) 24 Cal.4th 317, casts doubt on Sheppard, an at-will employment agreement does not give an employer the right to mislead an employee into moving across the country. The court denies summary adjudication on the fraud/concealment/nondisclosure claims. Defendant fails to maintain its burden of production. Defendant's separate statement of undisputed facts focuses on whether defendant misrepresented Mr. Simon's role as plaintiff's replacement. Defendant does not sufficiently address plaintiff's theory of liability based upon the relocation promises. Plaintiff's evidence establishes that defendant knew that plaintiff would not be relocated, but made misleading statements. (Plaintiff's additional facts, 41, 42, 44, Exs D & E to Thanawala dec.). A constructive fraud/concealment cause of action can be established without a special relationship where there is exclusive knowledge, partial suppression, or active concealment. See Witkin, Torts, CA Law, Vol. 5, sec. 798-799 (2005). Mr. Collins made misleading statements while knowing that the relocation was not verified. (Tentative Ruling Continues in Part 2 of 2) =(302/SRB)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN O'KANE VS. REGAN CARROLL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS , CGC-14-536884, CGC-14-543332 WITH CASE CGC-14-536879
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 26, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF STEPHEN O'KANE'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS CGC-14-536884 AND CGC-14-543332 WITH CASE NO. CGC-14-536879. Granted. Case no. CGC-14-536879 is the lead case and is consolidated with case no. CGC-14-536884 and case no. CGC-14-543332 for all purposes. Plaintiff establishes that common questions of fact and law are involved in all three cases. (See CCP sec. 1048.) Mr. Jadallah's argument in opposition that the declaratory relief case should not be consolidated because it is entitled to priority pursuant to CCP sec. 1062.3 is unavailing. Mr. Jadallah has not filed a motion establishing the necessity of a speedy trial, as required by CCP sec. 1062.3(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SRB)

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER JOHNSON VS. SH PROPERTY, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Jennifer Johnson'S Motion To Compel Defendant'S Responses To Discovery, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday June 26, 2015, Line 2, Plaintiff Jennifer Johnson's Motion To Compel Defendant's Responses To Discovery, And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. Defendants SH Property and Bohan shall provide responses to the discovery at issue by July 20, 2015. Sanctions of $350 are awarded against Defendants, but not their counsel. Defendants' counsel from Low, Ball and Lynch is not obligated to participate in the drafting of the discovery responses as Judge Quidachay has ruled that the firm may withdraw from representation after the hearing on this matter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL VS. TINA LOUISE JONES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 26, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANTS WILLIAM GINSBERG, BERG INJURY LAWYERS, INC., ROBERT DALBY, AND WILLIAM BERG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer is overruled. Through its police power the City and County of San Francisco has the power to "make and enforce within its limits all local, police, sanitary, and other ordinances and regulations not in conflict with general laws." (Cal. Const., art XI, Sec. 7.) Sec. 124 and 124.5 of the San Francisco Health Code is not duplicative and does not conflict with Government Code Section 23004.1, therefore the City ordinance is not preempted. Further, because Section 124.5(c) requires "the recipient's attorney retained to assert the action or claim shall provide written notice of such action or claim by personal delivery or first-class mail to the Bureau of Delinquent Revenue Collection in the Office of the Treasurer-Tax Collector within 10 days of asserting such action or claim" the ordinance does not violate the State Bar Act because no privilege or duty of confidentiality applies to information that has already been disclosed. [See Cal. Evid. Code Sec. 912, subd. (a).] Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SRB)
